skill.name: lark-wiki
skill.description: 飞书知识库：管理知识空间和文档节点。创建和查询知识空间、管理节点层级结构、在知识库中组织文档和快捷方式。当用户需要在知识库中查找或创建文档、浏览知识空间结构、移动或复制节点时使用。
skill.summary: 飞书知识库：管理知识空间和文档节点。创建和查询知识空间、管理节点层级结构、在知识库中组织文档和快捷方式。当用户需要在知识库中查找或创建文档、浏览知识空间结构、移动或复制节点时使用。
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell backticks, path traversal, weak cryptography, and entropy. Review found no evidence of weak cryptography, binary content, or malicious prompt injection, but the skill does instruct agents to use external lark-cli commands and read a parent-directory shared skill file. Publication is reasonable with a warning about trusted CLI setup and cross-file dependency review.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Command Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run lark-cli schema and lark-cli wiki commands. This is a legitimate integration pattern, but it executes local commands and may use authenticated Lark credentials, so the CLI binary and arguments must be trusted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly documents lark-cli command execution. The context is legitimate API tooling, but command execution is real and can affect authenticated Lark resources.

medium_findings.title: Parent-Directory Shared Skill Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to read ../lark-shared/SKILL.md before use. This appears intended for shared authentication guidance, but it reaches outside the skill folder and depends on external local content not present in this package.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory path is explicit and crosses the package boundary. The stated purpose is authentication and permissions, so it is not clearly malicious but warrants review.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Match Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported a weak cryptographic algorithm on the Chinese description line. Review found no cryptographic operation or algorithm reference on that line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 4 is descriptive text about Lark Wiki functionality. There is no evidence of hashing, encryption, or weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: Entropy Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported high entropy at the start of the Markdown file. Review found normal YAML front matter and readable Markdown content, not binary or encrypted data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file begins with standard front matter and remains human-readable throughout. No encoded payload or obfuscated content was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown command examples invoke lark-cli for schema inspection and wiki API calls. Treat parameters as sensitive because these commands can operate on authenticated Lark data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is directly present in a bash block. Risk depends on CLI provenance and how user-provided flags are formed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Parent Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The instruction references ../lark-shared/SKILL.md. This is a package-boundary concern because marketplace reviewers cannot verify that dependency from this skill alone.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The relative path is explicit. The surrounding text indicates a shared-auth dependency rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable access to Lark Wiki node information without guessing API fields. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to inspect schemas and call the Lark Wiki CLI correctly.
actual_capabilities: Guides use of lark-cli wiki commands for Lark Wiki API access.
Requires schema inspection before calling wiki API methods.
Documents the spaces.get_node method for retrieving wiki node information.
Identifies the wiki:node:read permission scope needed for node reads.
Directs agents to shared authentication and permission guidance before use.
limitations: Requires lark-cli to be installed and configured in the environment.
Only the spaces.get_node method is listed in the visible API resource table.
Depends on a shared Lark skill file outside this package for authentication guidance.
Does not provide built-in validation beyond lark-cli schema inspection.

use_cases.title: Inspect Wiki Nodes
use_cases.description: Retrieve metadata for a Lark Wiki node after checking the required command schema.

use_cases.title: Support Document Operations
use_cases.description: Prepare accurate Lark Wiki CLI calls when a support request includes a wiki node identifier.

use_cases.title: Audit Wiki Access Requirements
use_cases.description: Confirm that a workflow has the wiki:node:read scope before attempting node reads.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Node
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Lark Wiki skill to inspect the schema for spaces.get_node, then help me retrieve information for this wiki node: [node token].

prompt_templates.title: Verify Required Scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Before calling Lark Wiki, tell me which scope is required for spaces.get_node and whether my app permission list includes it: [permission list].

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a CLI Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the lark-cli schema for wiki spaces.get_node and draft the exact parameters I should provide for this node request: [request details].

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot Wiki Retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Lark Wiki guidance to diagnose why my spaces.get_node call failed. Check schema, required flags, permissions, and likely authentication issues.

output_examples.input: Find the information needed to read a wiki node.
output_examples.output: The assistant first checks the Lark Wiki command schema, confirms the node read scope, and lists the parameters needed for the request.

output_examples.input: Help troubleshoot a failed wiki node lookup.
output_examples.output: The assistant compares the request with the schema, checks whether wiki:node:read is available, and suggests correcting authentication or parameter values.

output_examples.input: Prepare a safe Lark Wiki CLI workflow.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines schema inspection, permission confirmation, command preparation, and result review without exposing credentials.

best_practices: Inspect the lark-cli schema before every Wiki API call.
Confirm the required Lark permission scope before running node operations.
Keep tokens and workspace identifiers out of shared prompts and logs.
anti_patterns: Do not guess --data or --params fields without checking the schema.
Do not run Lark Wiki commands with an untrusted lark-cli binary.
Do not expose app credentials, tenant tokens, or node tokens in public conversations.

faq.question: What does this skill help manage?
faq.answer: It helps agents work with Lark Wiki node operations through lark-cli guidance.

faq.question: Does it require external software?
faq.answer: Yes. The environment must provide lark-cli, and it must be configured for the target Lark workspace.

faq.question: Which Wiki method is documented?
faq.answer: The visible resource table documents spaces.get_node for retrieving knowledge space node information.

faq.question: What permission is required for node reads?
faq.answer: The skill lists wiki:node:read as the required scope for spaces.get_node.

faq.question: Can it create or move Wiki documents?
faq.answer: The description mentions broader Wiki management, but the visible method table only documents node retrieval.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it uses external CLI commands and a shared-file dependency.
